title: Keeping Computers Safe and Sound
story: Every November 30, people around the world celebrate National Computer Security Day by learning how to protect their devices from hackers and viruses. Kids and adults update their passwords, install security software, and learn about staying safe online. Schools often teach students about creating strong passwords and not clicking on suspicious links. Many families use this day to check their computers for updates and talk about internet safety rules. It's like giving your computer a superhero shield to fight off digital bad guys!
activity_question: What do people often update on National Computer Security Day?
follow_up_question: What makes a password really strong and hard for hackers to guess?

title: Mousse Day Makes November 30 Magnificent!
story: Fluffy, creamy, and absolutely delicious - that's what November 30 is all about on National Mousse Day! People celebrate by whipping up their favorite mousse recipes, from rich chocolate to tangy lemon varieties. Restaurants offer special mousse desserts, and home bakers get creative with layered parfaits and fancy presentations. Social media fills with photos of perfectly swirled treats that look almost too pretty to eat. Whether it's a simple chocolate mousse or an elaborate tiramisu-style creation, this day gives everyone the perfect excuse to indulge in one of the world's most elegant desserts.
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Mousse Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative mousse flavor combination you can imagine?

title: Stay Home Because You're Well Day
story: November 30 is Stay Home Because You're Well Day, when perfectly healthy people get permission to skip work or school just because they feel like it! This silly holiday encourages everyone to take a mental health break without needing to fake a cough or sneeze. People celebrate by sleeping in, watching movies, playing games, or doing absolutely nothing productive. Some families declare it a pajama day and order pizza for lunch. Others use it to catch up on hobbies they never have time for during busy weekdays.
activity_question: What do people do on Stay Home Because You're Well Day?
activity_choices: Take a break even though they're healthy
Go to the doctor for a checkup
Only stay home if they're sick
Work extra hard to make up for lost time
activity_answer: Take a break even though they're healthy
follow_up_question: What would be your perfect way to spend a guilt-free day at home?

title: Cities Light Up for Life
story: Cities around the world turn on special lights on November 30 to celebrate Cities for Life Day! This amazing day brings communities together to show they believe in second chances and hope. Towns light up their most famous buildings, bridges, and monuments in bright colors. People gather in public squares to share stories and support each other. It's a beautiful way to show that every life matters and deserves respect.
activity_question: What do cities do with their famous buildings on Cities for Life Day?
activity_choices: Light them up in bright colors
activity_answer: Light them up in bright colors
follow_up_question: What building in your city would look coolest lit up in bright colors?
